data.narrative:
Anysphere is a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Michael Truell, Sualeh Asif, Aman Sanger, and Arvid Lunnemark. The company has raised $3.2B+ to date and carries a current valuation of $29.3B. Valuation analysis relies on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables in the frontier AI space: OpenAI ($300.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), Neuralink ($9.7B), and Groq ($7.0B).

Peer-median methodology applied to this 4-company set yields an implied valuation of $96.3B. The peer range spans $7.0B to $300.0B, reflecting substantial dispersion driven by differences in model maturity, capital deployment, and perceived defensibility. This represents the primary triangulation method available given data constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $81.9B (low) to $110.8B (high), anchored at a median of $96.3B. Anysphere's current $29.3B valuation sits materially below this peer-derived range. Key uncertainties include the comparability of Anysphere's technology and market position relative to OpenAI and Anthropic at the high end, and the extent to which earlier-stage peers like Groq appropriately reflect frontier AI risk. Limited public comps (Palantir) and reliance on a single methodological pillar constrain confidence in the range. Additional data on revenue, user metrics, or burn rate would refine this assessment.
